evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build standards, documentation, and data catalogs for the organization
evidence.collaboration_width: Partner with business and technical stakeholders from problem framing to shipped artefacts
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design, own, and evolve core data models and the modelling architecture
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build standards, documentation, and data catalogs for the organization
evidence.autonomy_level: Design, own, and evolve core data models and the modelling architecture
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design, own, and evolve core data models and the modelling architecture; Build standards, documentation, and data catalogs for the organization
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Designing and evolving core data models spans new architecture work and ongoing iteration, while building standards and catalogs adds fresh foundational work, together supporting a mixed classification.
evidence.ai_role: Experience using AI-assisted coding or coding agents in a disciplined way (reviews, tests, documentation)
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD explicitly lists disciplined use of AI-assisted coding or coding agents as a desired skill, indicating the candidate is expected to use AI tools to accelerate their own work.
evidence.testing_framing: Build standards, documentation, and data catalogs for the organization
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Standards, documentation, and catalog work is described without an explicit testing tool or QA ownership verb, so it does not rise to an owned testing responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: GDPR compliance is named as a required qualification, signaling that regulatory/compliance risk is a dominant concern for this role.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals (standards, documentation, data catalogs, sound modeling judgment) with no velocity language present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: GDPR handling is a constraint; primary risk is business analytics accuracy for sales/marketing/product decisions, not financial reporting or regulatory compliance.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Language emphasizes evolving existing infrastructure and building standards, indicating an established team scaling rather than starting from zero.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The responsibilities section relies entirely on generic boilerplate action verbs and stakeholder collaboration language; it lacks specific tools, methodologies, technical depth, or Tuesday-morning context that would indicate hiring manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary focus on building data infrastructure and organizational standards indicates the role primarily serves the data function and its consumers rather than external business stakeholders.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Responsibility to design and own data architecture indicates strategic autonomy to define technical direction rather than execute direction set by others.
